applied.notes: Second round with Head of Product scheduled 6/28. Role owns the full quality bar for user experience on Render's cloud platform — from interaction design through production-ready React/TypeScript code. Not a prototyping role: you ship in the codebase. Requires 8+ yrs at the design-engineering intersection, exceptional React/TypeScript/CSS depth, ability to design independently in Figma AND in code, and strong craft instincts (typography, motion, spacing). Nice to have: animation/spring physics experience, open-source contributions, AI coding agent fluency. 6M+ developers on platform, Series C ($257M total). The portfolio alignment here is very strong.
applied.scoreRationale: Comp ceiling ($300K) exceeds target, fully remote, and the design-engineer hybrid scope is an exact match for a frontend engineer with strong design sensibility. Render's dev-tools domain and craft culture are direct portfolio alignment.

applied.notes: Recruiter screen done, moving to technical interview with Karri (CEO). Linear is fully async-remote. Small eng team (~25). TypeScript + React stack, strong code review culture. They expect engineers to care deeply about craft — this isn't a ticket-factory environment.
applied.scoreRationale: Linear's tooling domain is a direct portfolio match. Comp in range, async-first remote, and the team's craft standards align with track record. High signal from early recruiter conversation.

applied.notes: Applied via referral from former colleague. Role owns frontend infrastructure for vercel.com and the Dashboard — Next.js App Router, design system, performance. Strong portfolio angle with component library and LLM integration work.

Third round — system design panel with two senior engineers on 6/27. Trunk Tools is the leading AI platform for construction ($40M Series B, Insight Partners). Stack is TypeScript + Node + Express + React. Feature squad ownership model — drive new capabilities from concept to production. LLM-enabled agentic product, real users, hypergrowth. Passed coding screen (TypeScript refactor) and full-stack interview (React component through DB). NYC and Austin offices; free lunch in ATX office.
applied.scoreRationale: Real product impact and strong TypeScript/Node/React alignment. Comp ceiling ($215K) is below target but the agentic construction AI space is novel and the zero-to-one ownership model is compelling. Austin office is a commute bonus.

applied.notes: Applied via Greenhouse. Large-scale React/Node platform engineering. Storefront team owns the buyer experience for millions of merchants. Strong comp, remote, and the platform complexity (GraphQL, edge, internationalization) is a good stretch.
Declined after onsite — feedback was strong on system design but they prioritized a candidate with C++ / canvas rendering depth. Would reapply for an adjacent role (infra, design systems).

First recruiter call done. Notion's Developer Platform team owns the public API, blocks SDK, and integrations. Strong TypeScript/Node depth required. They're building out MCP server and AI agent integrations — exactly aligned with current work.
Offer received 6/21. Base $235K + equity. Deadline 6/29. Strong team and comp, but concerned about slow shipping cadence and heavy review culture at Stripe scale. Decision pending.
applied.scoreRationale: Top comp, great brand, strong engineering culture. Primary concern is Stripe's deliberate pace — may not match the shipping velocity preference.

prospect.notes: Remote. Building the Claude.ai web product — one of the fastest-growing AI interfaces in history. TypeScript/React frontend, strong emphasis on AI-native UX patterns. Mission-driven environment with extreme craft bar.
prospect.scoreRationale: Top-of-market comp, mission-driven, and building AI products daily with Claude is a once-in-a-career opportunity. Strong TypeScript/React alignment and AI integration track record make this a top-priority prospect.

prospect.notes: Remote. Deepgram is the leading Voice AI platform (50K+ developers, 200K+ orgs including Twilio and Cloudflare). Staff-level role on the Voice Agents team — ultra-robust ASR, real-time ordering agents, 3rd-party integrations (POS, loyalty, payments). Requires 7+ yrs, strong backend + cloud (Python/Go, AWS, Docker/K8s), AI/ML workflow comfort. Strong AI-first culture: AI tool use is measured and expected daily.
prospect.scoreRationale: Comp ceiling ($307K) is best in pipeline. Voice AI is a compelling technical domain. Backend + LLM integration focus is a strong portfolio match. AI-first operating rhythm aligns with current workflow. Very high priority.

prospect.notes: Remote (NYC HQ, new SF office). Valon is building the AI-native OS for regulated finance — $110B+ in mortgage servicing on their platform. Python + React + Docker + Kubernetes + GCP. Series C backed by a16z. Role requires 7+ yrs, strong system design, distributed systems experience. Fintech/regtech domain is adjacent but not a direct match.
prospect.scoreRationale: Comp is excellent ($255K–$300K). Regulated finance domain is a stretch but the engineering quality bar and a16z backing are strong signals. Worth a conversation to understand the actual day-to-day stack.

prospect.notes: Remote. Platform engineering for GitHub Copilot — the fastest-growing developer tool in history. TypeScript/Node + AI agent infrastructure. Direct alignment with AI-assisted dev workflow expertise.
prospect.scoreRationale: AI coding tooling domain is a perfect match. Building the infrastructure other developers use to write code is exactly the right leverage point. Comp and remote flexibility both strong.

prospect.notes: Remote. Core product platform (video recording, sharing, commenting). Acquisition by Atlassian adds stability but may slow pace. TypeScript/React frontend focus. Good comp and interesting async-video product domain.
prospect.scoreRationale: Comp is solid and the video product infrastructure is technically interesting. Main risk is post-acquisition pace concerns — same flag as on the design persona tracker.

prospect.notes: Remote. Large Java + React codebase. Comp floor below target. Enterprise CRM platform has strong scale but the tech stack (legacy Java backend) is a mismatch with TypeScript/Node strength.
prospect.scoreRationale: Comp below minimum and the Java-heavy backend is a poor stack match. CRM product domain is not compelling enough to compensate. Lower priority.

local.notes: Austin HQ, hybrid 2 days/week. Core product engineering for the job seeker experience — 350M monthly users. TypeScript/React frontend, Node.js backend, ML feature integrations. Large eng org with strong mentorship culture.
local.scoreRationale: Local hybrid within easy commute, strong comp, and the scale of the product (350M MAU) is a compelling engineering challenge. TypeScript/React stack is a direct match.

local.notes: Austin office. LangChain is the leading open-source LLM framework (90M+ downloads). DX role owns SDKs, documentation tooling, and developer onboarding experience. Strong alignment with AI/LLM integration expertise and developer tooling background.
local.scoreRationale: Local Austin role with strong LLM/AI platform alignment. Comp ($165K–$195K) is below top-of-market target but the product domain and open-source leverage are uniquely compelling. Worth a conversation.

local.notes: Round Rock — 18 miles. Hybrid 3 days/week. Large enterprise engineering org. Comp and pace both a mismatch. Good stability but not aligned with startup/product culture preference.
local.scoreRationale: Comp below target and enterprise pace is a culture mismatch. Good fallback if nothing else closes, but not top priority.

staffing.notes: Remote contract via Toptal network. TypeScript/React/Node engagements, typically 3-6 month terms with Series A-C product companies. Strong portfolio diversification and rate premium over FTE.
staffing.scoreRationale: Hourly rate ($100–$130/hr ≈ $200–$270K annualized) exceeds FTE floor. Toptal network quality is high. Good fallback option with quick start times.

staffing.notes: Decentralized talent network. AI-native product companies. Remote. Rate premium for LLM integration expertise. Current pipeline has 2 active matches in developer tooling space.
staffing.scoreRationale: Strong rate for AI integration expertise, remote, and the AI-focused client base is a direct match. Contract flexibility preferred over long FTE onboarding timelines.

passed.notes: Passed — PHP/Hack codebase and messaging-at-scale infrastructure is too far from product/DX focus. Layoff history in 2025 also a concern.
Comp below target. Java-heavy stack and enterprise culture mismatch. Passed.
IC comp floor. Java + Apex stack. Would revisit for a Staff-level opening in a TypeScript-forward team — domain overlap with enterprise AI is interesting.
Passed — organizational instability, unclear technical direction, and culture post-acquisition are all concerns. Role has been reposted multiple times.

pending.notes: Remote. WorkOS powers auth and identity for 500+ enterprise SaaS companies (OpenAI, Cursor, Perplexity, Vercel, Plaid). Engineers own Dashboard, Admin Portal, AuthKit, and WorkDS design system (Radix-based). Role contributes to design system in Figma and code, ships improvements directly to production, and engages customers via Slack. Requires 7+ yrs with complex B2B/developer tools, systems thinking, front-end code depth, and AI workflow curiosity.
pending.scoreRationale: Developer infrastructure domain with direct TypeScript/React alignment. Customer list (OpenAI, Cursor, Vercel) is excellent signal quality. Comp ceiling ($225K) is slightly below top-of-market target but the scope and growth trajectory justify exploring.

pending.notes: Remote-friendly (SF or NYC preferred). Substack is looking for engineers with LLM fluency for prototyping and potentially owning front-end product surfaces. Fast, low-process environment; engineers collaborate directly with founders. TypeScript/React preferred, SwiftUI a bonus. Strong AI tool adoption expected.
pending.scoreRationale: Unique startup role with strong TypeScript/React alignment and explicit LLM-fluency expectation. Comp below target but product mission (creator economy) and engineering culture are compelling. Worth a conversation.
Comp ($115–145K) falls well below target and the consumer coupon product domain is a poor fit for developer tooling and AI integration track record.
